decisionFlipScore.explanation: If customers successfully wielded leverage to prevent price elevation (assumption false), memory suppliers would face sustained pricing pressure that undermines the 'supercycle' narrative—particularly for Jukan and Dylan's analyses, which depend on sustained demand translating into healthy margins and continued capex investment. The supercycle thesis requires not just demand persistence but also supplier pricing power; if that power evaporates, the cycle contracts into a commodity downturn despite continued AI chip demand.
